name: Falcon 9
description: A partially reusable two-stage-to-orbit medium-lift launch vehicle designed and manufactured by SpaceX.

name: Falcon Heavy
description: A partially reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle designed and manufactured by SpaceX.

name: Starship
description: A fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle being developed by SpaceX, the tallest and most powerful ever built.

name: Saturn V
description: An American human-rated super heavy-lift launch vehicle used by NASA between 1967 and 1973 for the Apollo program.

name: Space Shuttle
description: A partially reusable low Earth orbital spacecraft system operated from 1981 to 2011 by NASA.

name: SLS Block 1
description: The Space Launch System is an American super heavy-lift expendable launch vehicle used for the Artemis program.

name: PSLV
description: Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle is an expendable medium-lift launch vehicle designed and operated by ISRO.

name: LVM3 (GSLV Mk III)
description: Launch Vehicle Mark-3 is an expendable medium-heavy lift launch vehicle developed by the Indian Space Research Organisation.

name: Soyuz
description: A family of expendable Russian and Soviet carrier rockets developed by OKB-1 and manufactured by Progress Rocket Space Centre.

name: Proton-M
description: An expendable Russian heavy-lift launch vehicle derived from the Soviet-era Proton.

name: Ariane 5
description: A European heavy-lift space launch vehicle developed and operated by Arianespace for the European Space Agency (ESA).

name: Ariane 6
description: The newest heavy-lift launch vehicle developed by ArianeGroup for the European Space Agency.

name: Delta IV Heavy
description: An expendable heavy-lift launch vehicle, the largest type of the Delta IV family.

name: Atlas V
description: An expendable medium-lift launch vehicle operated by United Launch Alliance.

name: Electron
description: A partially recoverable orbital launch vehicle developed by Rocket Lab for the small satellite market.

name: Long March 5
description: A Chinese heavy-lift launch vehicle developed by the China Academy of Launch Vehicle Technology (CALT).

name: Soyuz-U
description: An expendable launch vehicle, the most frequently flown orbital rocket in history.

name: Long March 3B
description: China's primary orbital workhorse for geosynchronous satellite launches.

name: Delta II
description: An expendable launch vehicle originally designed and built by McDonnell Douglas, with a highly reliable flight record.

name: Titan IV
description: A family of heavy-lift space launch vehicles developed by Martin Marietta for the U.S. Air Force and NASA.

name: H-IIA
description: An active expendable launch system operated by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries for the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency.

name: Vega
description: An expendable launch system developed by the Italian Space Agency and the European Space Agency for small payloads.

name: Antares
description: An expendable launch system developed to launch the Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station.

name: New Glenn
description: A heavy-lift orbital launch vehicle in development by Blue Origin, named after astronaut John Glenn.

name: Vulcan Centaur
description: A heavy-lift launch vehicle designed to meet the demands of the US Space Force's National Security Space Launch program.

name: Nuri (KSLV-II)
description: South Korea's first fully indigenous orbital launch vehicle, designed to carry payloads to low Earth orbit.
